title: dryopteris subarborea
intro: Dryopteris subarborea, also known as Eastern Woodfern, is a type of fern species native to North America, characterized by its fronds featuring triangular or lance-shaped leaflets. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dryopteris subarborea include its potential to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
